channel.title: FreeBSD — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by FreeBSD (freebsd).

item.title: CVE-2026-45254 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Incorrect libcap_net limitation list manipulation
item.description: In the case of the cap_net service, when a key present in the old limit was omitted from the new limit, the missing key was treated as "allow any" instead of being rejected. In certain scenarios, an application that had previously restricted a subset…

item.title: CVE-2026-45255 · 7.5 HIGH — Remote code execution via installer Wi-Fi access point scans
item.description: When bsdinstall or bsdconfig are prompted to scan for nearby Wi-Fi networks, they build up a list of network names and use bsddialog(1) to prompt the user to select a network. This is implemented using a shell script, and the code which handled netwo…

item.title: CVE-2026-39461 · 8.8 HIGH — select(2) file descriptor set overflow causes stack overflow
item.description: libcasper(3) communicates with helper processes via UNIX domain sockets, and uses the select(2) system call to wait for data to become available. However, it does not verify that its socket descriptor fits within select(2)'s descriptor set size limit…

item.title: CVE-2026-45253 · 8.4 HIGH — Missing validation in ptrace(PT_SC_REMOTE)
item.description: ptrace(PT_SC_REMOTE) failed to properly validate parameters for the syscall(2) and __syscall(2) meta-system calls. As a result, a user with the ability to debug a process may trigger arbitrary code execution in the kernel, even if the target process…

item.title: CVE-2026-45252 · 5.5 MEDIUM — Heap overflow in FUSE_LISTXATTR
item.description: When a fusefs file system implements extended attributes, the kernel may send a FUSE_LISTXATTR message to the userspace daemon to retrieve the list of extended attributes for a given file. The FUSE protocol requires the daemon to return a packed list…

item.title: CVE-2026-45251 · 7.8 HIGH — Kernel use-after-free via file descriptor syscalls
item.description: A file descriptor can be closed while a thread is blocked in a poll(2) or select(2) call waiting for that descriptor. Because the blocked thread does not hold a reference to the underlying object, this closure may result in the object being freed whi…

item.title: CVE-2026-45250 · 7.8 HIGH — Stack buffer overflow via setcred(2)
item.description: The setcred(2) system call is only available to privileged users. However, before the privilege level of the caller is checked, the user-supplied list of supplementary groups is copied into a fixed-size kernel stack buffer without first validating it…

item.title: CVE-2026-35547 · 8.1 HIGH 🌩️ — Heap overflow in libnv
item.description: When processing the header of an incoming message, libnv failed to properly validate the message size. The lack of validation allows a malicious program to write outside the bounds of a heap allocation. This can trigger a crash or system panic, and i…

item.title: CVE-2026-39457 · 7.8 HIGH — Stack overflow via select() file descriptor set overflow
item.description: When exchanging data over a socket, libnv uses select(2) to wait for data to arrive. However, it does not verify whether the provided socket descriptor fits in select(2)'s file descriptor set size limit of FD_SETSIZE (1024). An attacker who is able t…

item.title: CVE-2026-42512 · 8.1 HIGH 🌩️ — Remotely triggerable out-of-bounds heap write in dhclient
item.description: As dhclient is building an environment to pass to dhclient-script, it may need to resize the array of string pointers. The code which expands the array incorrectly calculates its new size when requesting memory, resulting in a heap buffer overrun. A…

item.title: CVE-2026-7164 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — pf can overflow the stack parsing crafted SCTP packets
item.description: Incorrect packet validation allowed unbounded recursion parsing SCTP chunk parameters. This can eventually result in a stack overflow and panic. Remote attackers can craft packets which cause affected systems to panic. This affects any system where p…

item.title: CVE-2026-7270 · 7.8 HIGH — Local privilege escalation via execve()
item.description: An operator precedence bug in the kernel results in a scenario where a buffer overflow causes attacker-controlled data to overwrite adjacent execve(2) argument buffers. The bug may be exploitable by an unprivileged user to obtain superuser privileges…

item.title: CVE-2026-42511 · 8.1 HIGH 🌩️ — Remote code execution via malicious DHCP options
item.description: The BOOTP file field is written to the lease file without escaping embedded double-quotes, allowing injection of arbitrary dhclient.conf directives. When the lease file is subsequently re-parsed by dhclient, e.g., after a system restart, an attacker-…

item.title: CVE-2026-6386 · 6.2 MEDIUM — Missing large page handling in pmap_pkru_update_range()
item.description: In order to apply a particular protection key to an address range, the kernel must update the corresponding page table entries. The subroutine which handled this failed to take into account the presence of 1GB largepage mappings created using the shm…

item.title: CVE-2026-5398 · 8.4 HIGH — Kernel use-after-free bug in the TIOCNOTTY handler
item.description: The implementation of TIOCNOTTY failed to clear a back-pointer from the structure representing the controlling terminal to the calling process' session. If the invoking process then exits, the terminal structure may end up containing a pointer to fre…

item.title: CVE-2026-4748 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — pf silently ignores certain rules
item.description: A regression in the way hashes were calculated caused rules containing the address range syntax (x.x.x.x - y.y.y.y) that only differ in the address range(s) involved to be silently dropped as duplicates. Only the first of such rules is actually loade…

item.title: CVE-2026-4747 · 8.8 HIGH — Remote code execution via RPCSEC_GSS packet validation
item.description: Each RPCSEC_GSS data packet is validated by a routine which checks a signature in the packet. This routine copies a portion of the packet into a stack buffer, but fails to ensure that the buffer is sufficiently large, and a malicious client can trigg…

item.title: CVE-2026-4652 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Remote denial of service via null pointer dereference
item.description: On a system exposing an NVMe/TCP target, a remote client can trigger a kernel panic by sending a CONNECT command for an I/O queue with a bogus or stale CNTLID. An attacker with network access to the NVMe/TCP target can trigger an unauthenticated Deni…

item.title: CVE-2026-4247 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — TCP: remotely exploitable DoS vector (mbuf leak)
item.description: When a challenge ACK is to be sent tcp_respond() constructs and sends the challenge ACK and consumes the mbuf that is passed in. When no challenge ACK should be sent the function returns and leaks the mbuf. If an attacker is either on path with an es…

item.title: CVE-2026-3038 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Local DoS and possible privilege escalation via routing sockets
item.description: The rtsock_msg_buffer() function serializes routing information into a buffer. As a part of this, it copies sockaddr structures into a sockaddr_storage structure on the stack. It assumes that the source sockaddr length field had already been validate…

item.title: CVE-2026-2261 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — blocklistd(8) socket leak
item.description: Due to a programming error, blocklistd leaks a socket descriptor for each adverse event report it receives. Once a certain number of leaked sockets is reached, blocklistd becomes unable to run the helper script: a child process is forked, but this ch…

item.title: CVE-2025-15576 · 7.5 HIGH — Jail chroot escape via fd exchange with a different jail
item.description: If two sibling jails are restricted to separate filesystem trees, which is to say that neither of the two jail root directories is an ancestor of the other, jailed processes may nonetheless be able to access a shared directory via a nullfs mount, if…

item.title: CVE-2025-15547 · 8.8 HIGH — Jail escape by a privileged user via nullfs
item.description: By default, jailed processes cannot mount filesystems, including nullfs(4). However, the allow.mount.nullfs option enables mounting nullfs filesystems, subject to privilege checks. If a privileged user within a jail is able to nullfs-mount directorie…

item.title: CVE-2025-14769 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — ipfw denial of service
item.description: In some cases, the `tcp-setmss` handler may free the packet data and throw an error without halting the rule processing engine. A subsequent rule can then allow the traffic after the packet data is gone, resulting in a NULL pointer dereference. Malic…

item.title: CVE-2025-14558 · 7.2 HIGH — Remote code execution via ND6 Router Advertisements
item.description: The rtsol(8) and rtsold(8) programs do not validate the domain search list options provided in router advertisement messages; the option body is passed to resolvconf(8) unmodified. resolvconf(8) is a shell script which does not validate its input. A…

item.title: CVE-2025-24934 · 5.4 MEDIUM 🌩️ — SO_REUSEPORT_LB breaks connect(2) for UDP sockets
item.description: Software which sets SO_REUSEPORT_LB on a socket and then connects it to a host will not directly observe any problems. However, due to its membership in a load-balancing group, that socket will receive packets originating from any host. This breaks t…

item.title: CVE-2025-0662 · 4.9 MEDIUM — Uninitialized kernel memory disclosure via ktrace(2)
item.description: In some cases, the ktrace facility will log the contents of kernel structures to userspace. In one such case, ktrace dumps a variable-sized sockaddr to userspace. There, the full sockaddr is copied, even when it is shorter than the full size. This ca…

item.title: CVE-2025-0374 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Unprivileged access to system files
item.description: When etcupdate encounters conflicts while merging files, it saves a version containing conflict markers in /var/db/etcupdate/conflicts. This version does not preserve the mode of the input file, and is world-readable. This applies to files that would…

item.title: CVE-2025-0373 · 6 MEDIUM — Buffer overflow in some filesystems via NFS
item.description: On 64-bit systems, the implementation of VOP_VPTOFH() in the cd9660, tarfs and ext2fs filesystems overflows the destination FID buffer by 4 bytes, a stack buffer overflow. A NFS server that exports a cd9660, tarfs, or ext2fs file system can be made t…

item.title: CVE-2024-45289 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Unbounded allocation in ctl(4) CAM Target Layer
item.description: The fetch(3) library uses environment variables for passing certain information, including the revocation file pathname. The environment variable name used by fetch(1) to pass the filename to the library was incorrect, in effect ignoring the option.…

item.title: CVE-2024-39281 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Unbounded allocation in ctl(4) CAM Target Layer
item.description: The command ctl_persistent_reserve_out allows the caller to specify an arbitrary size which will be passed to the kernel's memory allocator.

item.title: CVE-2024-51566 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — bhyve(8) NVMe driver to guest-induced infinite loops.
item.description: The NVMe driver queue processing is vulernable to guest-induced infinite loops.

item.title: CVE-2024-51565 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — bhyve(8) hda driver buffer over-read
item.description: The hda driver is vulnerable to a buffer over-read from a guest-controlled value.

item.title: CVE-2024-51564 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — bhyve(8) infinite loop in the hda audio driver
item.description: A guest can trigger an infinite loop in the hda audio driver.

item.title: CVE-2024-51563 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — bhyve(8) virtio_vq_recordon time-of-check to time-of-use race
item.description: The virtio_vq_recordon function is subject to a time-of-check to time-of-use (TOCTOU) race condition.

item.title: CVE-2024-51562 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — bhyve(8) nvme_opc_get_log_page buffer over-read
item.description: The NVMe driver function nvme_opc_get_log_page is vulnerable to a buffer over-read from a guest-controlled value.

item.title: CVE-2024-41721 · 8.1 HIGH 🌩️ — bhyve(8) out-of-bounds read access via XHCI emulation
item.description: An insufficient boundary validation in the USB code could lead to an out-of-bounds read on the heap, which could potentially lead to an arbitrary write and remote code execution.

item.title: CVE-2024-43102 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — umtx Kernel panic or Use-After-Free
item.description: Concurrent removals of certain anonymous shared memory mappings by using the UMTX_SHM_DESTROY sub-request of UMTX_OP_SHM can lead to decreasing the reference count of the object representing the mapping too many times, causing it to be freed too earl…

item.title: CVE-2024-32668 · 8.2 HIGH — bhyve(8) privileged guest escape via USB controller
item.description: An insufficient boundary validation in the USB code could lead to an out-of-bounds write on the heap, with data controlled by the caller. A malicious, privileged software running in a guest VM can exploit the vulnerability to achieve code execution o…

item.title: CVE-2024-45063 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Multiple issues in ctl(4) CAM Target Layer
item.description: The function ctl_write_buffer incorrectly set a flag which resulted in a kernel Use-After-Free when a command finished processing. Malicious software running in a guest VM that exposes virtio_scsi can exploit the vulnerabilities to achieve code execu…

item.title: CVE-2024-43110 · 8.4 HIGH — Multiple issues in ctl(4) CAM Target Layer
item.description: The ctl_request_sense function could expose up to three bytes of the kernel heap to userspace. Malicious software running in a guest VM that exposes virtio_scsi can exploit the vulnerabilities to achieve code execution on the host in the bhyve usersp…

item.title: CVE-2024-42416 · 8.4 HIGH — Multiple issues in ctl(4) CAM Target Layer
item.description: The ctl_report_supported_opcodes function did not sufficiently validate a field provided by userspace, allowing an arbitrary write to a limited amount of kernel help memory. Malicious software running in a guest VM that exposes virtio_scsi can exploi…

item.title: CVE-2024-8178 · 9.3 CRITICAL — Multiple issues in ctl(4) CAM Target Layer
item.description: The ctl_write_buffer and ctl_read_buffer functions allocated memory to be returned to userspace, without initializing it. Malicious software running in a guest VM that exposes virtio_scsi can exploit the vulnerabilities to achieve code execution on t…

item.title: CVE-2024-41928 · 8.4 HIGH — bhyve(8) privileged guest escape via TPM device passthrough
item.description: Malicious software running in a guest VM can exploit the buffer overflow to achieve code execution on the host in the bhyve userspace process, which typically runs as root. Note that bhyve runs in a Capsicum sandbox, so malicious code is constrained…

item.title: CVE-2024-45288 · 8.4 HIGH — Multiple vulnerabilities in libnv
item.description: A missing null-termination character in the last element of an nvlist array string can lead to writing outside the allocated buffer.

item.title: CVE-2024-45287 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Multiple vulnerabilities in libnv
item.description: A malicious value of size in a structure of packed libnv can cause an integer overflow, leading to the allocation of a smaller buffer than required for the parsed data.

item.title: CVE-2024-7589 · 8.1 HIGH 🌩️ — OpenSSH pre-authentication async signal safety issue
item.description: A signal handler in sshd(8) may call a logging function that is not async-signal-safe. The signal handler is invoked when a client does not authenticate within the LoginGraceTime seconds (120 by default). This signal handler executes in the context o…

item.title: CVE-2024-6759 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — NFS client accepts file names containing path separators
item.description: When mounting a remote filesystem using NFS, the kernel did not sanitize remotely provided filenames for the path separator character, "/". This allows readdir(3) and related functions to return filesystem entries with names containing additional pat…

item.title: CVE-2024-6760 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — ktrace(2) fails to detach when executing a setuid binary
item.description: A logic bug in the code which disables kernel tracing for setuid programs meant that tracing was not disabled when it should have, allowing unprivileged users to trace and inspect the behavior of setuid programs. The bug may be used by an unprivilege…

item.title: CVE-2024-6640 · 6.3 MEDIUM — pf incorrectly matches different ICMPv6 states in the state table
item.description: In ICMPv6 Neighbor Discovery (ND), the ID is always 0. When pf is configured to allow ND and block incoming Echo Requests, a crafted Echo Request packet after a Neighbor Solicitation (NS) can trigger an Echo Reply. The packet has to come from the sam…
